new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar produced by acetic fermentation of wine of fresh grapes, classified under heading 2209 of the Combined Nomenclature.

new_classes.label_de: Weinessig in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 2209 00 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine vinegar presented in containers with a nominal volume of not more than 2 litres, classified under CN code 2209 00 11.
Process by which wine of fresh grapes is converted into wine vinegar through acetic acid fermentation by Acetobacter bacteria.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for CN 2209 00 11 specifies: (1) the product is wine vinegar (distinguished from other vinegar types), (2) it is presented in containers of ≤ 2 L. These two criteria are captured by the new WineVinegarSmallContainer22090011 class, the AceticFermentationProcess distinguishing wine vinegar from other vinegars, and the SmallContainer restriction. However, the legal text also cross-references Additional Note 11 to Chapter 22 (which likely specifies analytical criteria for wine vinegar, e.g. acidity thresholds) and the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209 part (I) second paragraph (1) (which may further detail the composition or production requirements). These referenced notes are not available in the provided legal text, so their specific distinguishing criteria cannot be axiomatised. Coverage is therefore partial (≈0.72).
